prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Arr! Thar be two reasons why th' sky be blue, me hearties. Th' first be th' scatterin' o' light. See, when th' sun shines upon th' clouds, th' blue light gets scattered in all directions, makin' th' sky look blue t' us. An' th' second reason be th' rarin' o' air. When ye look up, th' blue color o' th' sky gets scattered in th' air, makin' it appear blue. So, there ye have it mateys, th' sky be blue 'cause o' scatterin' o' light an' rarin' o' air.
